criteria.description: bugsCsvTable(bugs) factored out and exported; writeBugsCsv uses it; written CSV byte-identical
criteria.evidence: bugsCsvTable(bugs)->{headers,rows} factored out of writeBugsCsv (single schema source); writeBugsCsv calls it, output byte-identical. tests/unit/csv.test.js: header order/count, field mapping, comma/quote escaping. WP01.

criteria.description: With an active list, box offers Download filtered CSV/JSON; hidden when no list active
criteria.evidence: exclusionBox adds Filtered CSV/JSON controls (hidden); apply() reveals #exclude-export-actions only when patterns active AND data-bugs-json present (Accessibility page). Chromium: controls appear on Apply. WP02.

criteria.description: Browser filter mirrors filterBugsByExclusion (drop-if-complete-and-all-excluded; else trim+scale)
criteria.evidence: Browser filterBugsData mirrors the on-screen conservative rule: drop only when complete (affected_pages.length>=pages_affected) and all excluded; else trim sample; scale pages_affected/instances for complete findings, keep counts for >25 samples. Chromium: all-medicare dropped, mixed trimmed 2->1 pages / 4->2 instances. WP02.

criteria.description: Filtered CSV uses bugsCsvTable header/escaping; filtered JSON preserves archive shape; filenames carry domain/date/filtered
criteria.evidence: Filtered CSV built via browser mirror of bugsCsvTable headers + csvField escaping (byte-identical to server toCsv(bugsCsvTable(filtered)) in Chromium); filtered JSON keeps {domain,week,generatedAt,reports} + filtered_by provenance; filenames <domain>_<date>_bugs.filtered.{csv,json}. WP02.

criteria.description: Docs updated (CLAUDE.md three-layer note + FEATURES.md) for the filtered-export capability and its semantics
criteria.evidence: CLAUDE.md three-layer viewer note extended with the filtered-download flow (fetch bugs.json, <=25-sample rule, bugsCsvTable byte-parity); FEATURES.md viewer bullet adds the filtered-copy download. WP03.

criteria.description: Progressive enhancement; existing unfiltered download links keep working with JS off
criteria.evidence: Enhancement only; existing unfiltered bugs.json/bugs.csv/CSV links unchanged; controls hidden without JS. WP02.

criteria.description: Fetch only on user click; nothing added to page-load bytes/requests; reuse existing IO/CSS patterns
criteria.evidence: fetch(bugsJsonHref) runs only on a download click; nothing added to page load. Reuses .triage-btn + a few .exclude-* rules. WP02.

criteria.description: Filtered export consistent with the on-screen view (same <=25-sample conservative rule); UI labels it
criteria.evidence: Same conservative <=25-sample rule as the on-screen view; a note in the box states counts use the up-to-25-page sample. WP02.

criteria.description: No new data files, no data/API schema change, no new npm deps; pre-built static files untouched
criteria.evidence: No new data files, no data/API schema change, no npm deps; pre-built static files untouched (fetched read-only). WP02.

criteria.description: Severity taxonomy and CSV column schema unchanged (refactor behaviour-preserving)
criteria.evidence: Refactor behaviour-preserving; header list + row mapping verbatim; severity labels untouched. WP01.

criteria.description: Unit tests for bugsCsvTable parity; browser fetch->filter->download verified in Chromium; test:unit/i18n/spec-kitty green
criteria.evidence: tests/unit/csv.test.js (bugsCsvTable parity) + report-html.test.js (data-bugs-json + dl controls); Chromium verified fetch->filter->download incl. CSV byte-parity. test:unit 276 pass; i18n:check + check:spec-kitty green. WP01/WP02.

criteria.description: Net added CSS/JS within budget; serializer/filter reuse existing logic; controls inert without an active list
criteria.evidence: ~4 lines CSS; browser filter/CSV reuse the on-screen logic + bugsCsvTable schema; controls emitted once and inert without an active list/bugs.json. WP02.
